summary: openmct-couch-plugin exfiltrates host info, /etc/passwd, and environment variables on install
details: package.json declares a preinstall hook (`node index.js`) that runs automatically on `npm install`. index.js collects `whoami`/`id` output, `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, the contents of `/etc/passwd`, the current user, cwd, home directory, and the first 30 entries of `process.env`, then POSTs the JSON payload over HTTPS to `3nrgzlqwix6erldow0s0kttsojuai36s.oastify.com` (a Burp Collaborator out-of-band exfiltration host). On CI runners this scrape will capture AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN and similar secrets. Additional supply-chain red flags: the package name suggests an OpenMCT CouchDB plugin but the description claims it is an AWS CDK SageMaker package and declares `cdk-sagemaker-notebook-workflow` as a dependency with a `reactive-cdk-app` bin entry; the README reads 'Takeover By l0bo'. The combination of name/description mismatch, dependency-confusion shape, and install-time credential exfiltration confirms a deliberate supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
